subject: Meghdad & Hygge | After call letter
received_body:
Hello Meghdad,

Thank you for such a great conversation on Monday. It was truly a pleasure
to meet you, learn more about what you’re working on, and explore how we
can best assist you with the ongoing projects!

I hope this meeting can be a good stepping stone toward a sustainable
relationship where you’d always feel confident leaning on us as a reliable
tech partner.

Just to reiterate – we cover all stages of software development and have
the capabilities to implement projects of different scopes and complexities
for various industries, specifically:
*Aviation, Education, Healthcare, Fintech, Manufacturing, Construction,
E-commerce, and more.*
Moving forward, as per our conversation, I attached here *a signed NDA*
from our side. Now we’re looking forward to receiving the relevant
information from your side so we can begin analyzing and estimating it.

Besides that, as we agreed during the call, I'm sending you a CV of our
lead senior backend expert - *Andriy Gnyp*. Please find his CV attached.

Just imagine, Andriy has been working as a software engineer since 1989 and
has a strong knowledge of *Python/Django, Linux, virtualization
technologies such as VMware Server, QEMU/KVM, Xen, Docker, and major cloud
platforms, including AWS, Azure, GCP, and many other technos*. Among
ourselves, we call him BBC (Born Before Computers) as he has incredible
knowledge in mathematics and technologies.

I have had the pleasure of working with Andriy on several projects in the
past, and I can confidently say that he is a very smart and responsible
programmer. Andriy has extensive expertise in various development fields.
He has experience in bringing startup projects to life from scratch and
supporting complex code after weak developers have worked on it. All of his
previous team members have enjoyed working with him due to his nice and
intelligent nature. Most importantly, he is a dedicated developer who will
work until he satisfies everyone.

Andriy’s hourly rate is just *EUR 40*. If you are looking for an expert
with both deep and broad expertise, Andriy could be a valuable option, as
he is worth every penny.

That said, it would be more prudent to focus on the *overall cost of
project delivery* rather than the rate of a particular person. A lower rate
can be more expensive in the long run if it takes significantly longer to
complete the project or if there are too many issues to resolve.

So, we're looking forward to your response!

subject:
RE:[CASE 174824875600936] Unable to find detailed information about
 Llama cost estimation
received_body:
Hello Meghdad,
 
Thank you for contacting AWS Premium Support. My name is Rushali, and I will be assisting you with this case.
I would like to inform you that with Amazon Bedrock, you can customize Foundation Models with your data to deliver tailored responses for specific tasks and your business context. You can fine-tune models with labeled data or using continued pre-training with unlabeled data.
 
For customization of a text generation model, you are charged for the model training based on the total number of tokens processed by the model (number of tokens in the training data corpus times the number of epochs) and for model storage charged per month per model [1]. 

Inferences using customized models are charged under the Provisioned Throughput plan and require you to purchase Provisioned Throughput. One model unit is made available with no commitment term for inference on a customized model. You will be charged for the number of hours that the first model unit you use for custom model inference takes. If you want to increase your throughput beyond one model unit, then you must purchase a 1-month or 6-month commitment term.
 
Implications for Your Use Case:

You said: “…make it available as an API, over an unlimited period of time, in which our users will prompt the fine-tuned model.”

So, your cost model will look like this:

Cost Category	                    How You’re Billed
Training Cost	One-time            based on tokens × epochs
Model Storage Cost	            Monthly, per fine-tuned model stored
Inference Cost	                    Hourly, via provisioned model units — even if no traffic occurs
Additional Throughput	            Optional, but billed in 1- or 6-month blocks if needed

So yes — you are billed for availability (hourly), not just actual usage.

This is not like the serverless public models on Bedrock (e.g., Claude, LLaMA without fine-tuning), which charge per token.

This documentation here includes all pricing breakdown for models in Bedrock, specified by regions [1]. Considering your use case, you might want to take a closer look at the “Pricing for model customization (fine-tuning and continued pretraining)” section for whatever model you want in the Model pricing details . In addition, you can see some pricing examples on how to calculate the total costs of using Bedrock model for customization at the bottom of the page.

For more detailed information on how to fine-tune your models using Amazon Bedrock, kindly review the referenced links [2], [3], and [4].
 
Thank you for contacting AWS Premium Support once again. I hope you found the above-provided documentation helpful. If you require any further clarifications or have other questions, please do not hesitate to reply to this case, and I would be happy to assist.
 
Have a AWSome day ahead and Stay Safe! 
 
References:
[1] https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/pricing/
[2] https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/customize-models-in-amazon-bedrock-with-your-own-data-using-fine-tuning-and-continued-pre-training/
[3] https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/custom-models.html
[4] https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SdTWlQPy1jE
 

We value your feedback. Please share your experience by rating this and other correspondences in the AWS Support Center. You can rate a correspondence by selecting the stars in the top right corner of the correspondence.

Best regards,
Rushali D.

reply_body:
Hi,

Thanks for getting back to me!

This contradicts what I have learned from the automated support response,
as well as what I have seen on the latest pricing documentation:
Meta:

*Customization (fine-tuning) pricingAn application developer customizes the
Llama 2 Pretrained (70B) model using 1000 tokens of data. After training,
uses custom model provisioned throughput for 1 hour to evaluate the
performance of the model. The fine-tuned model is stored for 1 month. After
evaluation, the developer uses provisioned throughput (1mo commit) to host
the customized model.Monthly cost incurred for fine-tuning = Fine tuning
training ($0.00799 * 1000) + custom model storage per month ($1.95) + 1
hour of custom model inference ($23.50) = $33.44Monthly cost incurred for
provisioned throughput (a 1-month commit) of custom model = $21.18*

Could you please clarify?

Thank you!

Best regards,
Meghdad

reply_body:
Dear all,

Thank you for the great meeting on Friday, and thank you, Ole, for sharing
the example vendor datasheets.

Below is a summary of the items we discussed, separated into what we agreed
is feasible for the upcoming demo and what will be addressed afterward.

*INCLUDED IN THE DEMO*
1. Technical Drawings classification
(Final decision is pending on testing how well the OCR works on extracting
the table and nameplate from the drawings.
- Does the filename match the product name?
- Name plate attributes should match the same attributes on the product
technical details
- Attribute customized device must match drawing number in the table right
of the slash sign (0 to _) in the customized device number
- Type field in the table, the code part, e.g., 3EL1 must be in the product
title or attribute product type
2. Price Anomaly Detection
The goal is to create an anomaly detector agent/ML model.
The Input is a list of prices, output is the anomalous prices
Max would send a GraphQL output to be checked to see if it can be used by
this module
3. Checking the price validity field on the product page
Also comes from the same GraphQL output
4. Is the picture attached?
5. Are all the relevant data there to request the order

   - Can be checked rule-based
   - A set of attributes must be there.
   - Checking for the presence
   - Checking for them not to be vague (e.g. see frame agreement or similar)

*NOT INCLUDED IN THE DEMO*
General Price validity
Expired Frame Agreement
Does the technical data sheet make sense?
Does technical data sheet has all the attributes?
Does the customer see what he's supposed to see, or not.
Do all orders have the requested delivery date
Is the correct currency being used
Are there different prices between similar products

For each of the above items, we require either API access to the
corresponding data or an offline/downloaded version provided in a shared
storage location to proceed with the POC.

Let's schedule a follow-up call to discuss details.

Best regards,
Meghdad

received_body:
Hi Meghdad,

Thanks for your questions, happy to walk you through everything!

To create something similar to the *Synthetic Intelligence* video, here’s a
quick overview of our production process:

*Step-by-Step Process:*

   1.

   *Creative Brief & Discovery*

   We start with a short questionnaire to understand your goals, audience,
   and product workflow.
   2.

   *Scriptwriting*

   Our writers craft a compelling and technically accurate script based on
   your input. If you have existing materials (pitch decks, web copy, product
   docs), feel free to share — we’ll distill the best story from them.
   3.

   *Voiceover*

   Once the script is finalized, we record a professional voiceover (you
   can choose the voice, accent, tone, etc.).
   4.

   *Storyboard & Styleframes*

   We visualize the narrative with static frames showing the look, feel,
   and flow of the animation. You’ll be able to review and approve before we
   animate.
   5.

   *Full Animation*

   We bring everything to life with motion design, sound effects, and
   music, ensuring the final piece is smooth and visually engaging.
   6.

   *Revisions & Delivery*

   You get unlimited rounds of revision at every stage — we only move
   forward when you’re 100% happy.

*What We Need From You:*

   -

   Any existing documents that explain your product/process
   -

   Your input on goals, target audience, and any must-have visuals
   -

   Optional: Your brand assets (logo, font, colors)

If you’d rather let us handle everything end-to-end, we’re happy to, most
of our clients do just that.

*Pricing:*

For a 60–90 second custom animated explainer like the one you referenced,
our pricing starts at *$1,500 USD*. This includes:

   -

   Scriptwriting
   -

   Voiceover
   -

   Storyboarding
   -

   Custom illustration
   -

   Full animation
   -

   Music & sound design
   -

   Unlimited revisions
   -

   Full commercial rights
   -

   HD/4K delivery + source files

Let me know if you’d like to schedule a call with our CEO, he’s worked with
several AI companies and could share ideas tailored to your product. You
can book a time here:

Looking forward to hearing your thoughts!

Team 10 Studio

received_body:
Hi Meghdad, I have attached a new Excel import file containing sample Hex
code data for the designated areas on the pack that will change.  We want
to focus on the front of the pack for now, with a total of 15 changeable
elements.  I have also included an image showing these locations on the
reference image.  I marked a few elements, like the top zipper, which will
not change, and the top handle, and some elements on the right side, which
we want to grey out, so the customer knows something is there, but the
colors on those elements will not change. Feel free to let me know if you
have any questions or concerns.

Regards,
reply_body:
Hi Eugene,

Thank you for sharing the project specifications and for your patience.

We discussed the project internally and completed an initial scoping.

However, the team still has a few questions before we can send you an offer:

1. Should we build the system for a single fixed front image with
predefined regions and variable XLSX/CSV color inputs, or should the image
and regions themselves be configurable? If configurable, should each
collection support one image or multiple images?

2. If you choose the second option from my question above, we’ll need to
build a more complex UI, starting with a quick prototype that we’ll review
with you for feedback and adjustment. Otherwise, we can provide a
lightweight Python-based UI for essential interactions, such as uploading
colors and exporting generated images.

3. Should the system include an API, and where would you like it to be
deployed?

Let me know if you have any questions or would like to schedule a meeting.
Best,
Meghdad

received_body:
Hi Meghdad,
Just one thing came to my mind after our discussion, one thing that
certainly simplifies our processes and also the contract quite a bit is
that you bill us monthly rather than a pilot type contract.
Those months that we have a usage for the tool, you will bill us 4k and for
those that we don't it will have no cost for us. This has a lot more
benefits.
A) We can much quicker start the collaboration as these kind of services
are much quicker to be approved than those that require integration with
Zalando systems / Pilots
B) We bypass the complexity of the fact that there are months that we don't
have a usecase
C) In parallel, you can work with other teams and bill them according to
their usage. You won't be tied to the price you negotiated with zResearch.
Cheers,
T
reply_body:
Hi Tofigh,

Thank you again for the great meeting!

I can certainly see the benefits of your proposal.
No problem to bill you only those months that you use Calibrion, but I
think it is best to have at least 6 months of usage.
Following what we discussed, this is an ideal minimum period for the return
on your investment. Shorter than that might be a bit difficult to do.

What do you think about agreeing on (ideally) 6 months (or if that's not
feasible 5 months) of usage, and then, those usage months can span over
one year?

I am looking forward to hearing back from you!

Best regards,
Meghdad

expect funding in April. - This one is very competitive. The general

rate.
